annotations.label: Ordinal number

anchor.snippet: is a generalization of ordinal numerals

annotations.note: `Ordinal` is defined as the quotient of well-orders by order-isomorphism.

annotations.label: Comparability of well-ordered sets

anchor.snippet: either they are isomorphic

annotations.note: For any two well-orders, one is an initial segment of the other (and isomorphism is the equality case).

annotations.label: Well-order

anchor.snippet: is termed a well-order

annotations.note: `IsWellOrder` is the typeclass for a relation that is trichotomous, transitive and well-founded.

annotations.label: Ordinals as isomorphism class representatives

anchor.snippet: are defined as the representative forms

annotations.note: `Ordinal.type` maps a well-order to its order-isomorphism class, the ordinal.

annotations.label: Omega

anchor.snippet: to be the least element that is greater than every natural number

annotations.note: `Ordinal.omega0` (ω) is the order type of ℕ, the least infinite ordinal.

annotations.label: Least element of a collection of ordinals

anchor.snippet: has a unique least element

annotations.note: Well-foundedness of `<` on `Ordinal` gives every nonempty collection a least element.

annotations.label: Open interval has no least element

anchor.snippet: any open interval has no least element

annotations.note: This is an illustrative remark about dense orders, not stated as a Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: Well-ordered set (ZF)

anchor.snippet: a well-ordered set is a totally ordered set

annotations.note: `IsWellOrder` captures a total order in which every nonempty subset has a least element.

annotations.label: Order isomorphism

anchor.snippet: is called an order isomorphism

annotations.note: `OrderIso` (and `RelIso`) is an order-preserving bijection between ordered sets.

annotations.label: Order type of a well-ordering

anchor.snippet: is called the order type of the well-ordering

annotations.note: `Ordinal.type r` is the order type of the well-order `r`.

annotations.label: Order-isomorphism is an equivalence relation

anchor.section: Definition of an ordinal as an equivalence class
anchor.snippet: is an equivalence relation on well-ordered sets

annotations.note: The `Setoid` instance `Ordinal.isEquivalent` is precisely order-isomorphism as an equivalence relation on well-orders.

annotations.label: Order type as isomorphism class (Principia)

anchor.section: Definition of an ordinal as an equivalence class
anchor.snippet: is identified with its isomorphism class

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Ordinal` as the quotient (isomorphism class) of well-orders, exactly this notion.

annotations.label: Mostowski collapse lemma

anchor.snippet: is known as the Mostowski collapse lemma

annotations.note: Mathlib has the order-embedding collapse into an initial segment, but not the general set-theoretic Mostowski transitive collapse by name.

annotations.label: Finite von Neumann ordinals

anchor.snippet: are defined recursively as

annotations.note: `ZFSet.omega` is the set of finite von Neumann ordinals, built from `∅` (omega_zero) and `insert x x` (omega_succ).

annotations.label: Ordinal (von Neumann, formal)

anchor.snippet: is an ordinal if and only if

annotations.note: `ZFSet.IsOrdinal` is the von Neumann definition: a transitive set well-ordered by `∈`.

annotations.label: Omega as smallest inductive set

anchor.snippet: is usually defined as the smallest inductive set

annotations.note: `ZFSet.omega` is defined directly from ℕ and shown inductive (omega_zero/omega_succ), but not characterized as the smallest inductive set.

annotations.label: Elements of an ordinal are ordinals

anchor.snippet: is found by finding that all the relevant ordinals are elements

annotations.note: `ZFSet.IsOrdinal.mem` states every element of an ordinal is itself an ordinal.

annotations.label: Characterization of the order relation

anchor.snippet: has an alternative characterization

annotations.note: For ordinals, `x ⊆ y ↔ x = y ∨ x ∈ y`, i.e. `≤` is `⊆` and `<` is `∈`.

annotations.label: Ordinals are totally ordered and well-ordered

anchor.snippet: It is in fact a total order , and a well-order

annotations.note: `Ordinal` has a `LinearOrder` instance and `Ordinal.lt_wf`/`WellFoundedLT` makes it a well-order.

annotations.label: Union of a set of ordinals is an ordinal

anchor.snippet: If A is a set of ordinals, then

annotations.note: Any (small) set of ordinals is bounded above, so its `sSup`/`iSup` (the union) is again an ordinal.

annotations.label: Existence of a greater ordinal

anchor.snippet: there exists another ordinal greater than all of them

annotations.note: Every ordinal is below its successor, so the successor of the supremum of a set exceeds all its members.

annotations.label: Burali-Forti paradox

anchor.snippet: is not a set; otherwise

annotations.note: `not_small_ordinal` is the formal content of Burali-Forti: `Ordinal` is not a small type (a proper class).

annotations.label: Every well-ordered set has a unique order type

anchor.snippet: is order-isomorphic to exactly one ordinal

annotations.note: `type r = type s ↔ Nonempty (r ≃r s)` gives existence and uniqueness of the order type.

annotations.label: Three types of ordinals

anchor.snippet: is one of three types

annotations.note: Every ordinal is zero, a successor, or a (successor) limit.

annotations.label: Zero ordinal

anchor.snippet: is the least ordinal

annotations.note: `0 : Ordinal` exists and `Ordinal.zero_le` shows it is the least ordinal.

annotations.label: Successor ordinal

anchor.snippet: is a successor if

annotations.note: Ordinals have a `SuccOrder` instance; `Order.succ o` is the successor ordinal (`= o + 1`).

annotations.label: Limit ordinal

anchor.snippet: is a limit ordinal if

annotations.note: `Order.IsSuccLimit` (used for ordinals) is the limit-ordinal predicate.

annotations.label: Characterization of nonzero limit ordinals

anchor.snippet: and for every ordinal

annotations.note: `isSuccLimit_iff` characterizes a limit ordinal as nonzero with no immediate predecessor.

annotations.label: Limit ordinal is supremum of smaller ordinals

anchor.snippet: is equal to the supremum of all ordinals strictly less than it

annotations.note: A (pre)limit ordinal equals the supremum of all ordinals strictly below it.

annotations.label: Omega is the least limit ordinal

anchor.snippet: It is the least limit ordinal because each natural number

annotations.note: `omega0_le_of_isSuccLimit` shows ω ≤ any limit ordinal, i.e. ω is the least limit.

annotations.label: Decreasing sequences of ordinals are finite

anchor.snippet: must be finite

annotations.note: Well-foundedness of `<` precludes infinite strictly decreasing sequences of ordinals.

annotations.label: No infinite descending chains

anchor.snippet: has no infinite strictly descending chains

annotations.note: `Ordinal.lt_wf` (the `WellFoundedLT` instance) means there are no infinite descending chains.

annotations.label: Termination of procedures

anchor.snippet: is useful for proving termination for any procedure

annotations.note: This is a methodological remark; Mathlib uses well-founded recursion but no theorem states this application.

annotations.label: Transfinite sequence

anchor.snippet: indexed sequence of elements of

annotations.note: Ordinal-indexed sequences are represented as functions/bounded families, with no dedicated 'transfinite sequence' type.

annotations.label: Class-indexed sequence is a proper class

anchor.snippet: is a proper class

annotations.note: Mathlib does not reify proper classes, so a sequence indexed by all ordinals being a proper class is not stated.

annotations.label: Limit of an increasing transfinite sequence

anchor.snippet: its limit is defined as the least upper bound

annotations.note: The supremum `iSup`/`sSup` of an ordinal family is its least upper bound (the limit).

annotations.label: Continuous transfinite sequence

anchor.snippet: is said to be continuous

annotations.note: Continuity (preserving suprema at limits) is bundled into `Order.IsNormal` rather than defined separately.

annotations.label: Normal sequence

anchor.snippet: is called normal if it is both strictly increasing and continuous

annotations.note: `Order.IsNormal` means strictly monotone and continuous (sup-preserving at limits).

annotations.label: Transfinite induction

anchor.snippet: is true of all ordinals

annotations.note: `Ordinal.induction` is strong (transfinite) induction on ordinals.

annotations.label: Transfinite recursion

anchor.snippet: is defined by transfinite recursion on the ordinals

annotations.note: `Ordinal.limitRecOn` is the zero/successor/limit recursor for ordinals.

annotations.label: Existence and uniqueness of recursive functions

anchor.snippet: The existence and uniqueness of such a function are proven

annotations.note: `limitRecOn` plus its `_zero/_succ/_limit` computation rules give existence and (definitional) uniqueness.

annotations.label: Ordinal exponentiation by recursion

anchor.snippet: is defined recursively on

annotations.note: Ordinal exponentiation is the `Pow Ordinal Ordinal` instance defined via `limitRecOn`.

annotations.label: Normal functions have arbitrarily large fixed points

anchor.snippet: has arbitrarily large fixed points

annotations.note: `not_bddAbove_fp` shows the fixed points of a normal function are unbounded.

annotations.label: Classes of ordinals can be indexed

anchor.snippet: can be indexed by ordinals

annotations.note: `Ordinal.enumOrd` enumerates an unbounded set of ordinals in increasing order.

annotations.label: Additively indecomposable ordinal

anchor.snippet: that is not the sum of two strictly smaller ordinals

annotations.note: `IsPrincipal (·+·) a ↔ ∀ b<a, ∀ c<a, b+c ≠ a` is exactly additive indecomposability.

annotations.label: Epsilon numbers

anchor.snippet: These are called the

annotations.note: `Ordinal.epsilon = veblen 1` enumerates the epsilon numbers (fixed points of ω^·).

annotations.label: Cantor normal form

anchor.snippet: provides a standardized way of writing ordinals

annotations.note: `Ordinal.CNF` computes the base-ω Cantor normal form of an ordinal.

annotations.label: Natural operations on ordinals

anchor.snippet: are an alternative way to combine ordinals arithmetically

annotations.note: Natural (Hessenberg) sum/product of ordinals is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Initial ordinal of a cardinal

anchor.snippet: is called the initial ordinal of that cardinal

annotations.note: `Cardinal.ord c` is the least ordinal of cardinality `c`, i.e. its initial ordinal.

annotations.label: Axiom of choice and well-ordering

anchor.snippet: is equivalent to the statement that every set can be well-ordered

annotations.note: The well-ordering theorem is proved from Lean's choice axiom, but the equivalence AC ⇔ well-ordering is not formalized.

annotations.label: Infinite initial ordinals are limit ordinals

anchor.snippet: it is always a limit ordinal

annotations.note: `isSuccLimit_ord` shows `ord c` is a limit ordinal whenever `ℵ₀ ≤ c`.

annotations.label: Smallest uncountable ordinal

anchor.snippet: is the smallest uncountable ordinal

annotations.note: `ω₁ = Ordinal.omega 1` is defined and documented as the first uncountable ordinal.

annotations.label: Cofinality

anchor.snippet: is the order type of a cofinal subset of

annotations.note: `Ordinal.cof` is the least cardinality of a cofinal subset (cardinal-valued cofinality).

annotations.label: Countable limit ordinals have cofinality omega

anchor.snippet: any countable limit ordinal has cofinality

annotations.note: A limit ordinal below ω₁ has cofinality ℵ₀.

annotations.label: Cofinality of successor ordinals

anchor.snippet: the cofinality of any successor ordinal is 1

annotations.note: `cof_succ : cof (succ o) = 1`.

annotations.label: Regular ordinal

anchor.snippet: is called regular and it is always an initial ordinal

annotations.note: Mathlib defines regular cardinals (`Cardinal.IsRegular`); there is no standalone 'regular ordinal' predicate.

annotations.label: Cofinality is idempotent

anchor.snippet: So the cofinality operation is idempotent

annotations.note: `cof_ord_cof : o.cof.ord.cof = o.cof` is the idempotence of cofinality.

annotations.label: Unbounded (cofinal) subset

anchor.snippet: is said to be unbounded (or cofinal) in

annotations.note: `IsCofinal s : ∀ x, ∃ y ∈ s, x ≤ y` is the unbounded/cofinal subset notion.

annotations.label: Limit point

anchor.snippet: is a limit point of

annotations.note: `Ordinal.IsAcc o S` is `AccPt o (𝓟 S)`, i.e. `o` is a limit point of `S`.

annotations.label: Closed set

anchor.snippet: if it contains all of its limit points below

annotations.note: A set of ordinals is closed iff closed under suprema, equivalently it contains its limit points.

annotations.label: Club set

anchor.snippet: is commonly referred to as a club set

annotations.note: There is no club (closed-unbounded) predicate in Mathlib (only the separate closed and unbounded notions).

annotations.label: Set of limit ordinals is a club

anchor.snippet: is a club set, as there is always a limit ordinal

annotations.note: Not formalized, since club sets are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Range of a normal function is club

anchor.snippet: is a closed unbounded subset of

annotations.note: Mathlib links normal enumerations with closed sets, but does not state the range of a normal function is a club.

annotations.label: Intersection of club sets is club

anchor.snippet: is also a club set

annotations.note: Not formalized; a Mathlib TODO note even flags this as future work.

annotations.label: Closed unbounded filter

anchor.snippet: known as the closed unbounded filter

annotations.note: The club filter is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stationary set

anchor.snippet: is termed stationary if it has a non-empty intersection

annotations.note: Stationary sets are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mahlo cardinal

anchor.snippet: is defined as a Mahlo cardinal precisely when

annotations.note: Mahlo cardinals are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weakly Mahlo cardinal

anchor.snippet: as weakly Mahlo if it is weakly inaccessible

annotations.note: Weakly Mahlo cardinals are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Club filter is not an ultrafilter

anchor.snippet: is not an ultrafilter under the standard

annotations.note: Not formalized, as the club filter itself is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Partition into disjoint stationary sets

anchor.snippet: can be partitioned into

annotations.note: Solovay's stationary-partition theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unbounded and closed classes of ordinals

anchor.snippet: is unbounded if it contains arbitrarily large ordinals

annotations.note: `Unbounded (·<·)` (with order-topology closedness) covers subsets of `Ordinal`; dedicated proper-class versions are not formalized.

annotations.label: Stationary class

anchor.snippet: is stationary if it intersects every closed unbounded class

annotations.note: Stationary classes are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Epsilon-zero

anchor.snippet: is the smallest satisfying the equation

annotations.note: `ε₀` is defined (`ε_ 0`) and characterized as the least fixed point of `ω^·`.

annotations.label: Church–Kleene ordinal

anchor.snippet: which is the smallest ordinal that is not a computable ordinal

annotations.note: The Church–Kleene ordinal / computable ordinals are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Order topology is discrete iff at most omega

anchor.snippet: is discrete if and only if it is less than or equal to

annotations.note: No discreteness characterization of the ordinal order topology by the bound ω is present.

annotations.label: Open subsets of omega plus one

anchor.snippet: is open in the order topology if and only if either it is cofinite

annotations.note: This specific characterization of open sets in ω+1 is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Derived set

anchor.snippet: is the set of limit points of

annotations.note: `derivedSet A = {x | AccPt x (𝓟 A)}` is the set of limit points.

annotations.label: Cantor's derived set theorems

anchor.snippet: then there is an index α such that

annotations.note: Mathlib has the Cantor–Bendixson theorem (closed = perfect ∪ countable) but not the transfinite derived-set/rank iteration.

annotations.label: Cantor's second theorem (countable ordinal)

anchor.snippet: then there is a countable ordinal α such that

annotations.note: The countable Cantor–Bendixson rank result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: First and second number classes

anchor.snippet: the first number class

annotations.note: Cantor's 'number classes' terminology/objects are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cardinality of the second number class

anchor.snippet: is the first uncountable cardinality

annotations.note: `card_omega` gives `card ω₁ = ℵ₁`, the cardinality of the countable ordinals, though 'number class' is not named.

annotations.label: Number classes correspond to aleph numbers

anchor.snippet: correspond one-to-one with the aleph numbers

annotations.note: The aleph/initial-ordinal correspondence is formalized via `Cardinal.aleph`/`Ordinal.omega`, but not phrased as number classes.
